fault_hypothesis: On line 75, the filter condition uses `<= end` (inclusive) instead of `< end` (exclusive), violating the half-open interval [start, end) contract and incorrectly including points at the end boundary.

constraints.intent: Every returned point must have a timestamp strictly less than end (exclusive upper bound).

spec.expr: all(start <= p[0] and p[0] < end for p in __cbfl_result)

constraints.intent: All points in the result must satisfy the half-open interval [start, end).

spec.expr: not any(p[0] == end for p in points) or all(p[0] != end for p in [q for q in points if start <= q[0] <= end] if p[0] == end)

constraints.intent: Points at exactly the end boundary should not be included in the filtered result.

spec.expr: all(p[0] >= start and p[0] < end for p in __cbfl_result)

constraints.intent: Returned list must only contain points within [start, end) — catching the inclusive-end bug at the return.
